title: The Laterally-Rotated Ridge

features.name: the lateral throat
features.desc-on-touch: you walk sideways into a canyon that opens both ways; the horizon rotates around you, and forward and sideways trade places

features.name: the rotated forest
features.desc-on-touch: trees lean all the same direction, their lean growing stronger as they recede; the path between them turns as you turn

features.name: the perpendicular stones
features.desc-on-touch: boulders arranged in an order that keeps shifting; you cannot walk in a straight line here, and yet you never backtrack

features.name: the turned-soil channel
features.desc-on-touch: earth torn by some sideways force, furrows running perpendicular to gravity; the soil remembers moving sideways as its natural direction

features.name: the rotational singing stones
features.desc-on-touch: rocks that hum in rotation; as you pass them, their pitch shifts in the direction you're walking, whether forward or sideways

features.name: the-lateral-compass-shrine
features.desc-on-touch: a standing stone carved with arrows that all point sideways; no matter how you turn it, or how you turn, the arrows still point to where you just came from and where you're going next

inscription: every direction here has already become sideways before you turn to face it

file.name: Survey_Attempts_Lateral_Terrain_Abandoned.txt
file.note: compass readings rotate by 90 degrees each time the surveyor's hand moves; notes degrade into increasingly sideways handwriting; final entry reads only: ←

exits.label: back toward the path where sideways was still climbing
deeper into the rotated landscape
exits.to: NEW: the-gyre-without-center, where all rotation orbits nothing

exits.label: toward terraces where the slope learns to spiral
